core_thesis: Heidegger reopens the question of Being by distinguishing Being from beings and taking Dasein, the entity for whom Being is at issue, as the proper site of inquiry ([Being and Time §2](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/2); [§4](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/4)). Dasein is not a detached subject but being-in-the-world, disclosed through care and being-toward-death ([§12](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/12); [§53](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/53)). Truth is first unconcealment rather than correspondence, so thought must attend to the clearing in which beings appear ([§44](cite://heidegger/being-and-time/44); [Parmenides, 'Aletheia'](cite://heidegger/parmenides/aletheia)).

cross_engagements.brief: Abhinavagupta touches the same substrate structure axis, but not in the same register. This section works it as `anti-essential-relationality` in the phenomenological register; Abhinavagupta approaches it through the metaphysical register.
Adi Sankara presses the same selfhood structure axis in the phenomenological register, but with the value `witness-self` rather than `dasein`.
Akalanka presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `plural-pramana-realism` rather than `phenomenological-reduction`.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `phenomenological-reduction`.

Bhartrhari presses the same semantic mediation axis in the semantic-linguistic register, but with the value `language-as-creative-manifestation` rather than `language-subordinate-to-non-propositional-knowing`.

Alfred North Whitehead touches the same temporal mode axis, but not in the same register. This section works it as `historical-disclosure` in the phenomenological register; Alfred North Whitehead approaches it through the metaphysical register.
Anandabodha presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `identity-with-ground` rather than `not-soteriological`.
